county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bastrop County formed 1837 from Municipality of Mina. County seat is Bastrop. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1883 courthouse fire destroyed many records. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Bastrop. 1883 courthouse fire destroyed many records — check TSLAC and GLO for backup copies. One of the original Texas municipalities. Bastrop County Historical Society. Strong German, Mexican, and African American communities. Lost Pines area. Early coal mining records. Stephen F. Austin's colony settlement records at GLO.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bastrop County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1837; County Clerk in Bastrop holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Bastrop County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1883 to present. Some gaps due to 1883 courthouse fire destroyed many records.

source_profiles.source_name: Bastrop County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1883. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Bastrop County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1883. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Bastrop County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Bastrop County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Bastrop County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Bastrop County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Bastrop County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Bastrop County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Baylor County formed 1858 from Fannin. County seat is Seymour. North Texas frontier region with ranching and Indian defense heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Seymour. North Texas frontier county organized 1879. Ranching heritage — Waggoner Ranch territory. Baylor County Historical Society. Check Fannin County for pre-organization records. Indian frontier settlement era.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Baylor County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Seymour holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Baylor County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1858 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Baylor County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1858. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Baylor County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1858. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Baylor County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Baylor County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Baylor County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Baylor County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.parent_county: San Patricio, Goliad, Refugio, Live Oak
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bee County formed 1857 from San Patricio, Goliad, Refugio, Live Oak. County seat is Beeville. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Beeville. South Texas ranching county. Bee County Museum. Chase Field Naval Air Station records (WWII). Check San Patricio, Goliad, and Refugio for pre-1857 records. Strong Mexican-American heritage. FamilySearch microfilm available.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bee County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1857; County Clerk in Beeville holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bell County formed 1850 from Milam. County seat is Belton. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Belton. Central Texas county, home to Fort Hood (now Fort Cavazos). Bell County Museum. Significant military records at NARA. Czech and German immigrant communities. Belton Female College records (1866-1920s). Check Milam County for pre-1850 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bell County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1850; County Clerk in Belton holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bexar County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Spanish municipality). County seat is San Antonio. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in San Antonio. THE key South Texas repository — records from Spanish colonial era (1731+). San Antonio missions records (Franciscan registers of baptisms, marriages, burials). Bexar Archives at UT-Austin (1717-1836) — essential for colonial/Mexican era. Texas GLO has empresario records. DRT Library at the Alamo. Archdiocese of San Antonio sacramental records. German, Czech, Polish, Mexican, and Canary Islander heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bexar County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in San Antonio holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1836. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Bexar County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Bexar County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Bexar County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Bexar County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Bexar County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Bexar County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Bexar County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Blanco County formed 1858 from Burnet, Comal, Gillespie, Hays. County seat is Johnson City. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Johnson City. Hill Country county. Lyndon B. Johnson National Historical Park preserves LBJ family records. German settlement heritage — Blanco and Round Mountain communities. Check Burnet, Comal, Gillespie, and Hays for pre-1858 records. Johnson family records at LBJ Presidential Library, Austin.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Blanco County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Johnson City holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Blanco County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Blanco County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Blanco County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Blanco County from 1858. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Borden County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Gail. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Gail. West Texas county organized 1891. One of Texas's least-populous counties. Ranching heritage. Borden County Historical Society. Check Bexar County for pre-organization records. Sparse early documentation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Borden County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Gail holds deed books

source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Gail
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1920. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bosque County formed 1854 from McLennan. County seat is Meridian. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Meridian. Central Texas county with strong Norwegian immigrant heritage — the 'Norse Settlement' at Clifton/Cranfills Gap. Norwegian church records (Our Savior's Lutheran). Bosque Memorial Museum. Check McLennan County for pre-1854 records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bosque County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1854; County Clerk in Meridian holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bowie County formed 1840 from Red River. County seat is Boston. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1890 courthouse fire destroyed early records.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Boston (formerly New Boston). 1890 courthouse fire destroyed early records — check TSLAC and Red River County for backups. Northeast Texas county, oldest area of Anglo settlement. Texarkana straddles TX/AR border — check Arkansas records too. Bowie County had significant African American plantation population.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Bowie County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1840; County Clerk in Boston holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Brazoria County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Brazoria). County seat is Angleton. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Angleton. Original Texas county — Stephen F. Austin's first colony (Old Three Hundred). Some of the oldest Anglo records in Texas from 1824+. Brazoria County Historical Museum. Major plantation county — extensive enslaved persons records. Sugar and cotton plantation records. Brazosport area petrochemical records (20th century).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Brazoria County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Angleton holds deed books
